title: No Pawn Wall, Just Mate
setup: White's pawns are all gone, but Black's pawn wall is still intact — and that wall is now a coffin.
hint: The pawn wall on f7-g7-h7 helps White, not Black. Trade the rooks.
explanation:
## The Idea

With no white pawns left, the white king is exposed — but the **race to the back rank is one-sided**: Black's king has no escape, White's does. {{Rxa8#}} is mate in one:

1. White's rook captures the Black rook on a8.
2. Check is delivered from the 8th rank.
3. Black's king on g8 is locked in by f7-g7-h7 (its own pawns).
4. No piece can recapture or block.

### Curious detail

The White king has no pawn cover — yet it is not Black to move. **It doesn't matter who is more exposed in theory; it matters whose turn it is in practice.** White moves first and ends the game.

> Tempo is more valuable than pawns when the position is decisive.
